summary:
I found the issue. Nginx was configured to proxy requests for `/api/` to `http://127.0.0.1:5001`, but the Flask application was listening on port `5000`. 

I updated the upstream proxy configuration in `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default` to point to the correct port (`5000`), and `test.sh` now passes successfully. All endpoints are returning a 200 HTTP status along with the expected payload.
